parentQuestion: An inflammation response would include which of the following?

scaffolds.q: Inflammation causes redness, heat, and swelling at an injury site.
scaffolds.explain: the cardinal signs of inflammation: redness (more blood), heat (increased metabolism), swelling (fluid leakage), pain (nerve stimulation), loss of function (damage). these aren't bugs, they're FEATURES. Your body screaming 'DAMAGE HERE, SEND HELP.'

scaffolds.q: Blood vessels can change diameter to control blood flow.
scaffolds.explain: Let me show you something beautiful. Blood vessels aren't rigid pipes — they have smooth muscle that can contract (vasoconstriction = less flow) or relax (vasodilation = more flow). This dynamic control lets your body redirect blood exactly where it's needed. this is Perfectly orchestrated.
